troubleshooting.title: Tkinter Not Found
troubleshooting.shortdesc: Portfolio Manager requires Tkinter (Python's standard GUI library). This error occurs most often when Python is installed via Homebrew on macOS.

condition.p: Launching Portfolio Manager produces one of the following errors:
condition.codeblock: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named '_tkinter' ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'tkinter'

cause.p: Python was installed via Homebrew, which does not include Tkinter by default.

step.cmd: Download and install the official Python 3.11 (or later) package for macOS from python.org.

info.p: The python.org installer includes Tkinter. Do not use the Homebrew formula.

step.cmd: Confirm that the new Python is the active version:

info.p: The output should show the python.org installation path (typically /Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/…).

step.cmd: Delete the existing virtual environment and relaunch:

cause.p: On Linux, Tkinter is a separate package not always installed with Python.

step.cmd: Install the Tkinter package for your distribution:
